summary: Completed e2e testing of PDF Export feature for TravelCRM. All backend API tests passed (8/8). All frontend UI tests passed - Export PDF button visible, clickable, and triggers successful PDF download.
seed_data_creation: Demo data reset via POST /api/demo/reset - includes 5 passengers with trips, calls, and WhatsApp messages
context_for_next_testing_agent: PDF Export feature is fully functional. Backend endpoint GET /api/passengers/{passenger_id}/pdf returns valid PDF with passenger data, trips, calls, and WhatsApp messages. Frontend button with data-testid='export-pdf-button' triggers download. PDF uses reportlab library and includes: personal info, trip history, call history, and WhatsApp messages.

backend_endpoints: GET /api/passengers/{passenger_id}/pdf - PASSED - returns valid PDF (starts with %PDF-)
GET /api/passengers/{passenger_id}/pdf - PASSED - returns correct Content-Type: application/pdf
GET /api/passengers/{passenger_id}/pdf - PASSED - returns Content-Disposition with filename
GET /api/passengers/{passenger_id}/pdf - PASSED - returns 404 for non-existent passenger
GET /api/passengers/{passenger_id}/pdf - PASSED - requires authentication (401 without token)
GET /api/passengers/{passenger_id}/pdf - PASSED - returns 404 for other user's passenger
frontend_components: Export PDF button on PassengerDetail page - PASSED - visible with data-testid='export-pdf-button'
Export PDF button text - PASSED - shows 'Exportar PDF' with download icon
Export PDF button click - PASSED - triggers PDF download
Success toast notification - PASSED - shows 'PDF descargado exitosamente' after download

pdf_content_verified.headers: Content-Type: application/pdf, Content-Disposition: attachment with filename
pdf_content_verified.size: Varies based on passenger data (more trips/calls = larger PDF)
